ActionLog.title: Action Log
ActionLog.description: Represents a log of an administrative action for security and accountability.

id.description: Unique identifier for the log entry.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the action occurred.

adminId.description: Identifier for the admin who performed the action (e.g., 'admin').

details.description: A descriptive string of the action that occurred.

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Represents an announcement made by a teacher or the school, visible on the announcements board.

authorId.description: Reference to the User (teacher or admin) who created the announcement. (Relationship: User 1:N Announcement)

content.description: The main text content of the announcement.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the announcement was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the announcement was last updated.

classIds.description: References to Classes that this announcement is relevant to. (Relationship: Class N:N Announcement)

schoolWide.description: Indicates if the announcement is for the entire school.

Assignment.title: Assignment
Assignment.description: Represents a classwork assignment given to students.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this assignment belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Assignment)

description.description: A detailed description of the assignment.

dueDate.description: The date and time the assignment is due.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the assignment was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the assignment was last updated.

maxPoints.description: The maximum possible points for the assignment.

Submission.title: Submission
Submission.description: Represents a student's submission for an assignment.

assignmentId.description: Reference to the Assignment this submission is for. (Relationship: Assignment 1:N Submission)

studentId.description: Reference to the User (student) who made the submission. (Relationship: User 1:N Submission)

submissionDate.description: The date and time the submission was made.

content.description: The content of the submission (e.g., file URL, text).

grade.description: The grade received for the submission.

teacherFeedback.description: Feedback given by the teacher for the submission.

Attendance.title: Attendance
Attendance.description: Represents a student's attendance record for a specific class.

id.description: Unique identifier for the attendance record.

studentId.description: Reference to the User (student) whose attendance is being recorded. (Relationship: User 1:N Attendance)

classId.description: Reference to the Class for which attendance is being recorded. (Relationship: Class 1:N Attendance)

date.description: The date of the attendance record.

status.description: The attendance status (e.g., Present, Absent, Tardy).

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents a course offered at the school.

teacherId.description: Reference to the User (teacher) who teaches the course. (Relationship: User 1:N Course)

classIds.description: References to Classes that belong to this course. (Relationship: Class N:N Course)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this class belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Class)

name.description: The name or section identifier of the class (e.g., 'Math 101 - A').

studentIds.description: References to Users (students) enrolled in this class. (Relationship: User N:N Class)

joinCode.description: A unique code for students to join the class.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application (student, teacher, admin).

role.description: The user's role (e.g., student, teacher, admin, parent).

status.description: The user's account status (e.g., pending, approved, rejected).

classIds.description: References to Classes that the user (student or teacher) is associated with. (Relationship: Class N:N User)

isAdmin.description: Custom claim indicating if the user is an admin.

Parent.title: Parent
Parent.description: Represents a parent or guardian of a student.

email.description: The parent's email address, must end with @thsparent.edu.hk.

studentIds.description: An array of student IDs associated with this parent. (Relationship: User 1:N User)

CourseSelection.title: CourseSelection
CourseSelection.description: Represents a student's course selection for an upcoming semester.

id.description: Unique identifier for the course selection record.

studentId.description: Reference to the User (student) who made the course selection. (Relationship: User 1:N CourseSelection)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course selected. (Relationship: Course 1:N CourseSelection)

semester.description: The semester for which the course is selected (e.g., 'Fall 2024').

selectionDate.description: The date when the course was selected.

ReportCard.title: Report Card
ReportCard.description: Represents a student's report card for a specific class and semester.

id.description: Unique identifier for the report card.

studentId.description: Reference to the User (student) this report card is for.

classId.description: Reference to the Class this report card is for.

teacherId.description: Reference to the User (teacher) who wrote the report.

semester.description: The semester for which the report card is issued (e.g., 'Fall 2024').

grade.description: The final grade for the class (e.g., 'A', 'B+').

status.description: The status of the report card (e.g., 'draft', 'pending_review', 'released').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the report card was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the report card was last updated.

SchoolExtensionActivity.title: School Extension Activity
SchoolExtensionActivity.description: Represents a School Extension Activity (SEA) course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SEA course.

name.description: The name of the SEA course.

description.description: A detailed description of the SEA course.

ageGroup.description: The target age group for the course.

term.description: The term the course is offered (e.g., 'Fall 2024', 'Full Year').

schedule.description: The meeting time and day for the course.

price.description: The price of the course in HKD.

params.name: actionLogId
params.description: Unique identifier for the log entry.

definition.description: School-wide announcements. `schoolWide` boolean indicates the announcement is for everyone.
Represents a class section with denormalized studentIds to avoid complex queries.
Announcements specific to a class. Includes denormalized 'classIds' for authorization independence.
Course selections made by a student.

params.name: courseSelectionId
params.description: Unique identifier for the course selection record.

params.name: reportCardId
params.description: Unique identifier for the report card.

definition.description: School Extension Activity (SEA) courses available for enrollment.

params.name: activityId
params.description: Unique identifier for the SEA course.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to address the issues of announcement updates, assignment creation/grading, course information updates, and course selection visibility. It prioritizes authorization independence and QAPs through denormalization and structural segregation.

**Authorization Independence:**
*   Admin roles are checked using custom claims (`isAdmin: true`) on the user's auth token. This is more efficient and secure than checking document existence.
*   Announcements include denormalized `classIds` and `schoolWide` flags to avoid requiring `get()` operations to determine visibility for users.
*   Submissions are stored under a path that includes both `assignmentId` and `studentId` for easy retrieval and permission validation.
*   Access to course selections uses path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/courseSelections/{courseSelectionId}`) so we don't need to store student information on course selections for security.

**QAPs:**
*   Segregation of data by user role and ownership allows for secure `list` operations. For example, listing assignments for a specific course is done within the `/courses/{courseId}/assignments` collection, and security rules can easily verify the requesting user is the course teacher or an enrolled student.

**Structure Explanation:**
*   `/actionLogs`: A new collection to store logs of administrative actions for accountability.
*   `/announcements`: School-wide announcements. The `schoolWide` boolean determines visibility.
*   `/classes/{classId}/announcements`: Class-specific announcements. Teachers associated with the class can create announcements. Students enrolled in the class can view them.
*   `/courses`: Courses offered by the school. Teachers who 'own' the course can manage its details and create assignments.
*   `/courses/{courseId}/assignments`: Assignments for a specific course.
*   `/assignments/{assignmentId}/submissions/{submissionId}`: Student submissions are nested under their associated assignment for easier retrieval and grading.
*   `/users/{userId}`: User profiles, containing basic information like name, email, and role.
*   `/users/{userId}/courseSelections`: Course selections made by students.  This ensures that only the student (or an admin) can view or modify their course selections.
*   `/users/{userId}/reportCards/{reportCardId}`: A new collection to store student report cards. This structure ensures that a student's report cards are directly tied to their user document, simplifying security rules for access control.
*   `/school-extension-activities`: A new collection for School Extension Activity (SEA) courses.
